claims.claim_text: In February 2024, Air Canada was held legally liable for chatbot-generated misinformation about a bereavement discount.
claims.span_hint: line 28 | section: Why This Module Matters
claims.rationale: The incident is real and citable via the tribunal decision Moffatt v. Air Canada, 2024 BCCRT 149, but the primary legal source is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: ReAct interleaves reasoning traces and task-specific actions, using external sources between steps.
claims.rationale: The ReAct paper directly describes interleaving reasoning traces with actions that gather information from external sources.

claims.claim_text: ReWOO decouples reasoning from observation and can reduce token consumption by planning tool use upfront.
claims.rationale: The ReWOO paper explicitly says the method detaches reasoning from observations and significantly reduces token consumption.

claims.claim_text: Tree of Thoughts explores multiple reasoning paths and self-evaluates them to choose better next steps.
claims.rationale: The ToT paper directly states that the framework considers multiple reasoning paths and self-evaluates choices.

claims.claim_text: Multi-agent debate dramatically reduces factual error rates.
claims.anchor_text: By pitting agents against one another and bringing in an impartial arbitrator agent, you dramatically reduce factual error rates.
claims.suggested_rewrite: By pitting agents against one another and bringing in an impartial arbitrator agent, you can sometimes surface disagreements and catch factual errors that a single-pass workflow might miss.
claims.rationale: This is a strong empirical performance claim without a cited benchmark or source matching the stated magnitude.

claims.claim_text: Kubernetes Jobs are `batch/v1` resources for one-off tasks that run to completion and stop.
claims.span_hint: line 1635 | section: The Complete Autonomous Agent
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes Jobs documentation explicitly says Jobs are one-off tasks that run to completion and shows `apiVersion: batch/v1`.

claims.claim_text: Kubernetes lets you declare CPU and memory limits via `resources.limits` for Pods and containers.
claims.span_hint: line 1635 | section: The Complete Autonomous Agent
claims.rationale: The resource management documentation directly documents `spec.containers[].resources.limits.cpu`, `spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory`, and pod-level equivalents.

claims.claim_text: A simple stateless chatbot query might consume about 500 tokens.
claims.span_hint: line 1757 | section: The Token Multiplication Problem
claims.anchor_text: A simple query to a regular stateless chatbot ("What's the weather?") might consume ~500 tokens.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A simple query to a regular stateless chatbot can be relatively cheap in tokens.
claims.rationale: The ~500-token figure is illustrative and not tied to a stable primary source or reproducible benchmark.

claims.claim_text: At premium model pricing, an autonomous agent can cost several times more per task than a simple stateless chat response.
claims.span_hint: line 1770 | section: The Token Multiplication Problem
claims.anchor_text: At premium model pricing, an autonomous agent can cost several times more per task than a simple stateless chat response, so token use needs to be budgeted explicitly.
claims.suggested_rewrite: At metered token pricing, an autonomous agent can cost materially more per task than a simple stateless chat response, so token use needs to be budgeted explicitly.
claims.rationale: The exact cost multiplier is workload- and vendor-dependent, but the general lesson that agentic workflows can increase metered inference cost is sound.

claims.claim_text: Voyager is a Minecraft agent that writes and reuses executable skills as it explores.
claims.rationale: The Voyager paper directly describes an embodied Minecraft agent with an ever-growing skill library of executable code.

further_reading.title: Lost in the Middle: How Language Models Use Long Contexts
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful background for the module’s memory section because it explains why longer context windows do not eliminate retrieval and memory design problems.

further_reading.title: ReWOO: Decoupling Reasoning from Observations for Efficient Augmented Language Models
further_reading.why_relevant: Direct primary source for the module’s planning discussion on upfront tool planning and token-efficiency trade-offs.

further_reading.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
further_reading.why_relevant: Concrete example of a long-horizon agent architecture with reusable skills, relevant to memory, planning, and autonomous behavior.

notes: Excluded code blocks, Mermaid diagrams, hands-on exercise steps, and Knowledge Check scenarios per schema. The Quality Metrics and Evaluation Metrics tables read as prescriptive targets rather than externally grounded benchmark facts; if the pipeline treats numeric targets as citation-bearing claims, review that section separately for editorial normalization.
